threadTitle: Things that instantly reveal you just moved here
body: You still think the 'Overground' is an extension of the Tube instead of a completely different world. Welcome to Hackney!
Last week, the 196 was packed with a bunch of teenagers debating the best chicken shops in Brixton. You could smell the spicy scents wafting from their bags.

threadTitle: When you knew you were actually living here
body: When I heard that random jam session on the corner of Landstraße and Flughafenstraße at 3 AM, I knew I was home.

threadTitle: Things Berlin does that nowhere else does
body: Only in Berlin will you find a party that starts at a U-Bahn station after a transit strike, with DJs spinning amidst confused commuters.

threadTitle: Real signs someone is from here vs just working here
body: You know someone's from here when they instinctively swerve to avoid the giant pothole on 24th that’s been there for years. Tourists just plow right in.

threadTitle: Muni moments that define this city
body: Saw a guy in a full tuxedo calmly pulling an inflatable flamingo onto the bus. Only in SF, right?

threadTitle: Things tourists get wrong about living here
body: Tourists think Gràcia is all hipster cafes, but many locals still get their bread from the old-school panaderies that have been here for decades.

threadTitle: How to spot a recent arrival
body: They’re the ones who still say 'bon dia' instead of just 'hola' when entering a bar. The locals barely raise an eyebrow anymore.

threadTitle: Things that instantly reveal you just moved here
body: You still call it 'the Overground' instead of just 'the train.' Once you blend in, it'll just be 'the DLR' for you, too.
Caught the N59 last night; the driver sang along to every tune on the radio. Brixton's nightlife never sleeps, not even on the buses.

threadTitle: When you knew you were actually living here
body: I realized I truly belong when I saw a whole group of kids playing soccer in the middle of the street while a bus honked but just waited patiently.

threadTitle: Things Berlin does that nowhere else does
body: Only in Kreuzberg can you find a currywurst stand with vegan options open till 4 AM. That’s the real Berlin night life.

threadTitle: Real signs someone is from here vs just working here
body: You know you’re local when you don’t flinch at the sound of a loud bang from BART—you’ve learned it’s just Tuesday in this city.

threadTitle: Muni moments that define this city
body: Just saw a guy with a pizza box waving Muni down like a taxi at Civic Center. Only in SF do we turn delays into dinner plans.

threadTitle: Things tourists get wrong about living here
body: They think 'siesta' means shops close all afternoon. Nah, we’re just waiting for the heat to pass before heading out after 6!

threadTitle: How to spot a recent arrival
body: They’re the ones still fumbling with their T-10 cards at the metro machines, staring blankly when the screen glitches.
